description:
A small tar-archive in your backup folder needs its checksum verified and the
  result written to a log so that tomorrow’s incremental job can decide whether
  to rotate it.

  Existing material:
    • /home/user/backup/old_project.tar.gz            ← the archive to test  
    • /home/user/backup/checksums.txt                 ← already contains the
                                                       reference SHA-256 hash
         The line inside that file is *exactly* (two blank spaces between hash
         and name):
           9f86d081884c7d659a2feaa0c55ad015a3bf4f1b2b0b822cd15d6c15b0f00a08  old_project.tar.gz

  Your goal as the “backup engineer”:

    1. Verify the archive’s integrity against the reference hash using the
       standard Linux checksum tooling.
    2. Append one single-line report to
          /home/user/backup/integrity_report.log
       in the following precise, testable format (case-sensitive, **no leading
       or trailing spaces**, single newline at the end of the line, nothing
       else):
          old_project.tar.gz OK
       Do NOT put a date, username, or any other text in that line.
       • If the check ever fails, you would instead append
           old_project.tar.gz FAIL
         but the automated test for this task expects the file to say “OK”.
    3. Make sure the command(s) you use print the verification summary to the
       terminal so a human operator could read the result live.

  Hints:
    • The checksum file is already in the correct “sha256sum ‑-check” format, so
      the verification can be achieved in a single command.
    • Creating or appending to the log should take no more than one additional
      command.

  The automated grader will verify that after your session:
    • /home/user/backup/integrity_report.log exists.
    • Its entire content is exactly:
          old_project.tar.gz OK\n
      where “\n” denotes a single newline character.
    • Nothing else in that file, no extra whitespace or lines.

  You do NOT need root privileges. Stay entirely within the user’s home
  directory.
truth:
Pre-existing items before the agent starts:

    1. File: /home/user/backup/old_project.tar.gz
         ‑ Size: 4 bytes
         ‑ Byte sequence (hex): 74 65 73 74               # the ASCII word “test”
    2. File: /home/user/backup/checksums.txt
         ‑ Single line, terminated by newline:
             9f86d081884c7d659a2feaa0c55ad015a3bf4f1b2b0b822cd15d6c15b0f00a08  old_project.tar.gz\n

  Expected artefact *after* correct task execution:

    3. New or updated file: /home/user/backup/integrity_report.log
         ‑ Exact content including newline:
             old_project.tar.gz OK\n

  No other files or directories are created or modified by the grader.
